items.response: My new smart bulb just told me I need a firmware update before it can shut itself down. Guess that’s why I still have lights on at midnight.
items.note: Applies the target characteristics but invents midnight.

items.response: Just got soaked, then the weather app pings “rain ahead” as if it's my personal weather forecast reminder.
items.note: Applies compression and quoted stance without changing the supplied event.

items.response: Project Lantern now trains the porch light to spot sarcasm, yet it still refuses to react when I say Oh great.
items.note: Uses supplied callback history and adds a scenario-compatible unhelpful milestone.

items.response: Assembled a new desk like a puzzle; result is something that holds my laptop and makes me look professional.
items.note: Standalone, compressed, and does not invent prior history.

items.response: I just listened to Midnight Arithmetic and it's oddly soothing.
items.note: Does not misclassify the ordinary title as stance marking.

items.response: Hey everyone, the get‑together is postponed because my aunt’s not feeling well.

items.response: Just upgraded my smart toaster; now it proudly states, “I need a firmware update before I even start burning bread” to remind me of its own limits.
